programme.desc: Kiri loves to swing on vines, but one day a vine breaks.

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Fruity Tapper loves fruit and uses its long pointed beak like a jackhammer to shake them out of trees. To complete the mission, B.O.T. must avoid being rattled to pieces by the Fruity Tapper’s tapping and keep out of the way of falling fruit!

programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: It’s a big day in the garden as the residents prepare for the Potting Shed Amateur Dramatics Society’s annual show. Betty the Mouse is practicing her singing and Sid the Snail is getting ready for a rap, but Fred the Pill Millipede is struggling with his routine. He loves to tap dance, but it’s not easy when you have as many legs as he does. The other performers have lots of advice for Fred, but finding confidence under pressure can be a real challenge! Can he overcome his nerves in time for the big show?

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a horse puzzle and a macro safari, and watch as Book Boy makes a merry-go-round.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join Phinehas and Charlie as they both wait for their newborn baby brother or sister to arrive into the world. Charlie’s mum packs a bag ready to go to hospital with dad and waves goodbye to Charlie and Grandma, whilst Phinehas’ family gets ready to pop a gender reveal balloon to find out if the next arrival will be a boy or a girl. Before the big moment, Charlie wraps up a cuddly elephant toy with his Grandma as a gift for his new brother or sister. After a long wait, it’s finally time for Charlie and Phinehas to meet their new siblings. Both babies travel home to their families and meet their big brothers for the very first time. Once Phinehas meets the new baby, he joins in with dad to register the birth of their new baby. Charlie meets a health visitor, who has come to visit to check on the baby and make sure it is staying healthy and strong.

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Tig has chicken pox and finds it difficult to get better so Woolly tries to help her recovery

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: No one can agree on what to play today, so they create Sausages - a two-headed dog - who involves them all in a game of Simon Says, and everyone gets stuck in

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: A talented young cook from the Midlands prepares a very special Bakewell tart for his friends

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: While Zoe helps Doug with his homework project, about Alaska, Simon and Jess take Waffle and Evie to the shops to buy new school shoes. However, Waffle causes chaos

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy tries to complete his chores quickly so he can go outside to play, but ends up making more mess and getting into trouble

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: hen Mack goes missing, Molly decides to become a great detective and with her sidekick Daisy she hunts the Big Hub for clues. Meanwhile, Mack is searching for Molly!

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: An African fable explaining how Hippo was left with no hair following a fire

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The Alphablocks use beatboxing, rock, opera and disco music when they sing the sounds of the letters of the alphabet

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Five is so good at hide-and-seek, she can find the others without looking up from her book. Plus, learning to count to five

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug go to Buckingham Palace where they meet Queen Victoria.

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: Zam invents a machine that makes it possible to enter other people's dreams

programme.title: Mister Maker at Home
programme.desc: Mister Maker is having trouble using his trusty Makerphone and there’s only one friend who will be best placed to help him. He video-calls his friend Jen from CBeebies’s Andy’s Aquatic Adventures, which leads to arty inspiration! Mister Maker makes a pretend computer out of lots of card, stickers, fun foam and buttons. Later, Mister Maker checks in on his friends, The Shapes. They are snoring away, so Mister M makes sure he leaves them to have a nice rest. Mister Maker then decides to dance like a Shape himself, which in turn inspires him to make some shape art - he turns an old sock into something special! Lastly, Mister Maker remembers all the fun he has had throughout the day.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a magic castle, and there's magic with The Great Tumbleeny!

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: After a fun waving game with the Tiddlytubbies, the Teletubbies enjoy watching children waving as they set off on a short boat trip. Later, the Teletubbies wave to the Tiddlytubbies who are off to Sleepybyes

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne meets Lulu and her pet dog Benji and together they head off to the vets to have Benji's dew claws removed. Meanwhile Rory travels to a safari park to join Paddy the vet who is examining one of the tigers

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we join four-year-old Alexa, her little brother Archie, and Dad as they visit their friends for a weekend in the countryside. Alexa leads everyone on a walk to the forest and they all get stuck in the mud as they splosh through the fields. Later, Alexa and friends enjoy a sleep-over on a big bouncy blow-up bed! They finish their weekend together with a trip to the seaside. Alexa has fun collecting pebbles on a beach but will she be able to run away from the waves so her feet don't get soggy?

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace looks back at some of her favourite machines that are electric, fast and useful

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: A hungry bookworm munches its way through Captain Captain's Pirate Handbook. Evias, Amy, Reegan and Prince play Shivering Timbers and Coconutty Raft before taking on the Shipwreck Rummage to try to win back Gem's jewels

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog and the squirrels arrange a puppet show to tell the story of a great adventure they had

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: After accidentally sending a slumbering Mr Tolly floating off to Owl Island, Peter immediately launches a rescue with his friends

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: It's Frozen Fun Day, and Love Monster is having trouble learning to ice-skate. He asks Elder Kitten, who is much better at it, to teach him. Elder Kitten agrees to show him how and tells him that the trick is to get back up after falling. Despite falling over again and again, Love Monster gradually gets better - but how will he do in the big show?

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate explores different ways to travel. She uses an underwater camera to see a boat propeller and an animation reveals how the propeller pushes the boat through the water

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz and Bob's junk stall sells out of everything, including Bob's robo-helmet! Bob is horrified

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Alice organises a relaxation class, and Molly helps get people to attend. However, just as everyone starts to relax, Bob and Moira start on some very noisy work

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle must impress on his first day as a trial school pupil but struggles to hold a pen.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran go ice skating. Whilst Gran Gran is a little nervous JoJo is a natural. With Jared's help Gran Gran soon gets the hang of it!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah decides that octagons are her favourite shape, and starts an eight person club to celebrate

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Little Nana and Lambkin are being noisy, so Lily Plant suggests they read a book quietly. (R)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small, wearing a rainbow striped scarf, catches seven coloured stones floating in space. The Iron Chicken has also found a strange metal disc something in space. Do the stones and the disc somehow fit? The answer lies in the colours of the scarf

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines eat their dinner in the Ninky Nonk, but bounce all around the garden

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Ronnie O'Sullivan and Laila Rouass, while Jose Pizarro and Paul A Young prepare dishes in the studio. Plus, archive clips and wine choices

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas take an entertaining look at Wales' fortunes at this year's Six Nations. They are joined by one of the world's most sought-after opera stars, Wales' very own Bryn Terfel, while Irishman Ed Byrne provides the laughs. Plus, the four regions of Wales battle it out in a series of challenges to see who can be crowned champions of the Sin Bin Showdown

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the midweek's Premier League action and look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Liverpool v Manchester City

programme.title: Eddie Butler's Six Nations
programme.desc: Eddie Butler looks ahead to the 2021 Six Nations in the company of former Wales international Sam Warburton, and speaks to Eddie Jones, Serge Betsen, George North and Nigel Owens

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents as a team of gym enthusiasts take on a punk rock singer and an actress at Lincoln Antiques Fair, with help from experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley assists a couple who have £375,000 to spend on a home in the north Dorset countryside, and while in the area, she visits the renowned Lyme Regis Boat Building Academy

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore what Britons ate during wartime and discover how some of the UK's favourite dishes have evolved from rationing. The duo also meet army cooks to find out what troops on the front line eat today, and prepare rabbit stew and carrot cake

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall looking for two things to save from the clutches of the tip crusher. With the help of artisan Norman Wilkinson, Sarah hopes to turn a profit

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich head to Hertfordshire to come up with design ideas for a large garden in Hemel Hempstead. Its owners, Carly and Andy, are keen to tackle an eyesore dating back to the Second World War

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ashes-winning captain Michael Vaughan, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, world champion speed-skater Elise Christie and Great Britain sprint great John Regis join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, Joel Dommett and Hannah Cooper Dommett take on Rickie Haywood Williams and Melvin Odoom

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. TV property expert Martin Roberts, author and TV presenter Fern Britton, actress and singer Victoria Ekanoye and TV presenter Arthur Williams answer questions on Thunderbirds, Mary of Teck, Pretty Woman and British Military Aircraft of the Second World War

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as former child stars take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Adrian Hall, Julie Dawn Cole, Lyndyann Barrass, David Peachey, Nick Bond-Owen, Jessica Forte, Sally Thomsett and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha decides to tell Jan she is pregnant, but she needs to find the right moment. The pair are then called to a domestic abuse incident. Lev grapples with the truth that is tearing his family apart, and Leon makes a friend in David when he offers to fix his broken-down car

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: DI Lindsay Denton is investigated when she survives the ambush of a police convoy in which three of her colleagues are killed and a witness is seriously injured. AC-12's commanding officer Ted Hastings assigns new recruit Georgia Trotman to work alongside Steve Arnott on the investigation, and suspicions are soon raised about Denton. Starring Adrian Dunbar, Keeley Hawes, Martin Compston and Jessica Raine

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Arsenal at Villa Park and Manchester United v Everton at Old Trafford. The Villans and Gunners will both have been hoping to boost their chances of qualifying for Europe with a victory, while United continued their quest for the title with a potentially yough test aggainst the Toffees

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell look ahead to Tampa Bay Buccaneers v Kansas City Chiefs in Super Bowl LV at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida

programme.title: The Place Beyond the Pines (2012)
programme.desc: A motorcycle stunt rider is reunited with his lost love and the son he has never met. He gives up his life on the road to support them, but ends up drawn into crime, attracting the attention of a troubled, ambitious cop and setting in motion a conflict that will have unforeseen consequences for both men's children. Crime drama, starring Ryan Gosling and Bradley Cooper

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Arsenal at Villa Park and Manchester United v Everton at Old Trafford. The Villans and Gunners will both have been hoping to boost their chances of qualifying for Europe with a victory, while United continued their quest for the title with a potentially yough test aggainst the Toffees

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Fabio and Isobel Avemaria think living by the sea is just what their young family needs after health scares and redundancy. They consider whether now is the right time to make a move to Norfolk. Presented by Chris Bavin

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Nick Hall assist two teams challenged to scour Newbury Showground to pick up bargains to sell for profit at an auction presided by Thomas Plant. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp meets curator Brendan Carr from the Reading museum to talk about Reading's famous three Bs industry - beer, bulbs and biscuits

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser hosts the final of Gospel Singer of the Year from Gorton Monastery in Manchester, with three vocalists battling it out for the title. The judges are Alexandra Burke, David Grant and Heather Small and there is also a performance by Shaun Escoffery, who sings his single River

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: John Inverdale presents a look ahead to Wales v Ireland at Principality Stadium, as both teams get their Six Nations campaigns under way

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Ireland (Kick-off 3.00pm). The opening round of fixtures is brought to a conclusion with this afternoon's match at Principality Stadium. Ireland scored four tries en route to a 24-14 victory when these teams met in last year's competition, with Jordan Larmour, Tadhg Furlong, Josh van der Flier, and Andrew Conway all crossing the try line at The Aviva Stadium. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Sam Warburton, Jamie Heaslip and Martin Johnson, and commentary by Eddie Butler

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is on Crompton Moor in the southern Pennines to discover what makes this site of biological interest such a special place. Battered by rain, wind and snow, she learns how a plant is helping to restore these uplands, and meets a community coming together to bring wildlife back to the hills. Adam Henson tries out a new app to show how contented his animals are, wildlife film-maker Richard Taylor-Jones shows viewers how to spot wildlife on their doorsteps and Tom Heap investigates the mental health crisis in rural areas

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where items assessed include a letter from President Dwight D Eisenhower, a collection of Scottish silver and a Chinese table that is one of the most valuable ever seen on the programme. There is also a set of mystery objects to test even the most knowledgeable armchair expert

programme.title: Musicals: The Greatest Show
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith hosts a celebration of the world of musical theatre from the London Palladium, with the host joining Amanda Holden to perform I Know Him So Well from Chess. There are also numbers from The Greatest Showman, Mamma Mia!, Hamilton, Hairspray, Funny Girl, The Phantom of the Opera and Dreamgirls, while international superstar Idina Menzel talks to West End legend Elaine Paige

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles attempts to set up new life in Paris with Marie-Andree, but his past catches up with him, and the web of lies that he has told her begins to unravel. As the couple attempt to close business deals in France, Herman's committed investigation closes in on them, and what's left of their relationship finally implodes

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Gabby Logan reviews the day's Premier League action, including Liverpool v Manchester CIty at Anfield and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Leicester City at Molineux. Liverpool prevailed 3-1 in last season's corresponding fixture en route to winning the league, but their title-defence has stuttered in recent weeks while the Citizens looked to be hitting top form. Leicester have also impressed lately and will have hoped to boost their own chances of topping the table at the end of the campaign with a victory over a Wolves side that have struggled for form since a serious injury to Raul Jimenez. Plus, Sheffield United v Chelsea, Tottenham Hotspur v West Ham United and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Tampa Bay Buccaneers v Kansas City Chiefs (Kick-off 11.30pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of American football's showpiece match, which this year takes place at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida. The Buccaneers are the first team in Super Bowl history to be playing in their own stadium. However, despite their home advantage, they face a tough task against the Chiefs, who are the defending champions and prevailed 27-24 when the teams met here during the regular season. With analysis from Osi Umenyiora, Jason Bell and Mike Carlson, and commentary by Jim Nantz and Tony Romo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits property developments in Wolverhampton and South Norwood, south-east London, while Martin Roberts looks round a three-bedroom semi-detached in Derby

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: The Parkers dream of balancing a new business venture with family time in the great outdoors, so sample a week in Dorset to try to tempt their grown-up children away from London

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: In Essex, police officers are doused in petrol and threatened with being set alight after a moped chase ends in mayhem. In Knutsford, Cheshire, an officer trying to catch a suspected drug dealer falls onto a train track, sustaining life-changing injuries.

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie is hungry for the truth, while Karen hopes Tom and Ella have turned a corner. Ruhma encounters a reluctant mother and her complicated family

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda go on a Wild West adventure to the horse-riding world of Rancho del Ray to investigate a credit card scam, only to discover a tragic history that leads to murder. Crime drama, starring Elen Rhys and Julian Looman

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a recently retired Essex couple to look for a home in Staffordshire on their budget of £550,000, hoping to find a detached property with a view. Sonali also visits a celebrated Victorian pottery that is now a a hub of art, design and training

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Four groups of dedicated farmers who have shown resilience and ingenuity, go up against each other bidding to win this year's Against the Odds accolade at the Annual British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Rachel Treadaway-Williams investigates a plumber whose charges seem rather steep, while Mo Jannah is on the case of a missing parcel containing a family's priceless memories. Steffan Powell finds out how accessible the latest gaming consoles are to people with disabilities

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise's disappearance leaves her family and friends concerned, especially Chelsea, who is in a state of panic. Max babysits for Abi, causing him to reminisce about his much-missed daughter and how his life has not worked out the way he would have wanted. Dotty pushes Kush to decide whether he wants the spare room in her house, while the call centre has a new starter - Big Mo

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Sara Pascoe, Dan Walker, Nicola Coughlan and Mr Motivator to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The competition reaches the halfway point, where the contestants are challenged by Mary Berry to prepare the ultimate Sunday roast. The only rule is that the meal must involve roasted meat and some form of potatoes - they are otherwise free to do whatever they want. Two cooks compete to produce the best pork crackling, while another adds a Morrocan twist. For the second task, Chris Bavin challenges the cooks to make banana bread without using flour, while the final round involves a classic French dessert

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The presenting skills of the contestants are tested as they host their own daytime magazine show Morning Glory, with Lorraine Kelly joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by star of stage and screen Felicity Kendal, who is making her musical theatre debut in Anything Goes. Brit Regé-Jean Page, star of the hit Netflix drama Bridgerton, also appears as does rock legend Dave Grohl, who is promoting the new Foo Fighters album Medicine at Midnight. Derry Girls actress Siobhan McSweeney idscusses becoming host of The Great Pottery Throw Down and comedian Alan Carr reflects on taking the helm as host of Interior Design Masters. Plus, singer and podcaster Jessie Ware performs her new single Remember Where You Are

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: At Golitha Falls, where the River Fowey tumbles down to the sea, Rick meets Dawn French, who has made Cornwall her home. In the shadow of Devonport Dockyard, he boards the world's busiest chain ferry to cross the wide River Tamar, the ancient watery boundary between Devon and Cornwall - along the way discovering an unusual event involving circus elephant. And he meets a man who thinks he has discovered the next superfood, which Rick used to cook up a delicious duck dish

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: The Rev Dr Doug Gay and Dr Roisin Coll of Glasgow University present from the foyer of BBC Glasgow, with music by Tim Edey and Ross Ainslie

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits property developments in Wolverhampton and South Norwood, south-east London, while Martin Roberts looks round a three-bedroom semi-detached in Derby

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Mark De-Lisser hosts the final of Gospel Singer of the Year from Gorton Monastery in Manchester, with three vocalists battling it out for the title. programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: John Inverdale presents a look ahead to Wales v Ireland at Principality Stadium, as both teams get their Six Nations campaigns under way

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Ireland (Kick-off 3.00pm). The opening round of fixtures is brought to a conclusion with this afternoon's match at Principality Stadium. Ireland scored four tries en route to a 24-14 victory when these teams met in last year's competition, with Jordan Larmour, Tadhg Furlong, Josh van der Flier, and Andrew Conway all crossing the try line at The Aviva Stadium. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Sam Warburton, Jamie Heaslip and Martin Johnson, and commentary by Eddie Butler

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Helen Skelton is on Crompton Moor in the southern Pennines to discover what makes this site of biological interest such a special place. Battered by rain, wind and snow, she learns how a plant is helping to restore these uplands, and meets a community coming together to bring wildlife back to the hills. Adam Henson tries out a new app to show how contented his animals are, wildlife film-maker Richard Taylor-Jones shows viewers how to spot wildlife on their doorsteps and Tom Heap investigates the mental health crisis in rural areas

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where items assessed include a letter from President Dwight D Eisenhower, a collection of Scottish silver and a Chinese table that is one of the most valuable ever seen on the programme. There is also a set of mystery objects to test even the most knowledgeable armchair expert

programme.title: Musicals: The Greatest Show
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith hosts a celebration of the world of musical theatre from the London Palladium, with the host joining Amanda Holden to perform I Know Him So Well from Chess. There are also numbers from The Greatest Showman, Mamma Mia!, Hamilton, Hairspray, Funny Girl, The Phantom of the Opera and Dreamgirls, while international superstar Idina Menzel talks to West End legend Elaine Paige

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: Charles attempts to set up new life in Paris with Marie-Andree, but his past catches up with him, and the web of lies that he has told her begins to unravel. As the couple attempt to close business deals in France, Herman's committed investigation closes in on them, and what's left of their relationship finally implodes

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Gabby Logan reviews the day's Premier League action, including Liverpool v Manchester CIty at Anfield and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Leicester City at Molineux. Liverpool prevailed 3-1 in last season's corresponding fixture en route to winning the league, but their title-defence has stuttered in recent weeks while the Citizens looked to be hitting top form. Leicester have also impressed lately and will have hoped to boost their own chances of topping the table at the end of the campaign with a victory over a Wolves side that have struggled for form since a serious injury to Raul Jimenez. Plus, Sheffield United v Chelsea, Tottenham Hotspur v West Ham United and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Tampa Bay Buccaneers v Kansas City Chiefs (Kick-off 11.30pm). Mark Chapman presents coverage of American football's showpiece match, which this year takes place at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida. The Buccaneers are the first team in Super Bowl history to be playing in their own stadium. However, despite their home advantage, they face a tough task against the Chiefs, who are the defending champions and prevailed 27-24 when the teams met here during the regular season. With analysis from Osi Umenyiora, Jason Bell and Mike Carlson, and commentary by Jim Nantz and Tony Romo

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles looks at surgical equipment from the past, courtesy of the Bath Medical Museum

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: In Essex, police officers are doused in petrol and threatened with being set alight after a moped chase ends in mayhem. In Knutsford, Cheshire, an officer trying to catch a suspected drug dealer falls onto a train track, sustaining life-changing injuries.

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: The Parkers dream of balancing a new business venture with family time in the great outdoors, so sample a week in Dorset to try to tempt their grown-up children away from London

programme.title: Coronavirus: Scotland's Response
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Scottish Government's briefing, with reaction, expert analysis and the very latest updates from Scotland and beyond on the pandemic

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie is hungry for the truth, while Karen hopes Tom and Ella have turned a corner. Ruhma encounters a reluctant mother and her complicated family

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda go on a Wild West adventure to the horse-riding world of Rancho del Ray to investigate a credit card scam, only to discover a tragic history that leads to murder. Crime drama, starring Elen Rhys and Julian Looman

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a recently retired Essex couple to look for a home in Staffordshire on their budget of £550,000, hoping to find a detached property with a view. Sonali also visits a celebrated Victorian pottery that is now a a hub of art, design and training

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Four groups of dedicated farmers who have shown resilience and ingenuity, go up against each other bidding to win this year's Against the Odds accolade at the Annual British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Bournemouth for young married couple Thomas and Rhiann. They have a budget of £3,000 to create a shady, flower-filled space in their sunny back garden and Tom has a lifelong dream of putting in a massive koi carp pond

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise's disappearance leaves her family and friends concerned, especially Chelsea, who is in a state of panic. Max babysits for Abi, causing him to reminisce about his much-missed daughter and how his life has not worked out the way he would have wanted. Dotty pushes Kush to decide whether he wants the spare room in her house, while the call centre has a new starter - Big Mo

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Sara Pascoe, Dan Walker, Nicola Coughlan and Mr Motivator to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The competition reaches the halfway point, where the contestants are challenged by Mary Berry to prepare the ultimate Sunday roast. The only rule is that the meal must involve roasted meat and some form of potatoes - they are otherwise free to do whatever they want. Two cooks compete to produce the best pork crackling, while another adds a Morrocan twist. For the second task, Chris Bavin challenges the cooks to make banana bread without using flour, while the final round involves a classic French dessert

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The presenting skills of the contestants are tested as they host their own daytime magazine show Morning Glory, with Lorraine Kelly joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by star of stage and screen Felicity Kendal, who is making her musical theatre debut in Anything Goes. Brit Regé-Jean Page, star of the hit Netflix drama Bridgerton, also appears as does rock legend Dave Grohl, who is promoting the new Foo Fighters album Medicine at Midnight. Derry Girls actress Siobhan McSweeney idscusses becoming host of The Great Pottery Throw Down and comedian Alan Carr reflects on taking the helm as host of Interior Design Masters. Plus, singer and podcaster Jessie Ware performs her new single Remember Where You Are

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Ronnie O'Sullivan and Laila Rouass, while Jose Pizarro and Paul A Young prepare dishes in the studio. Plus, archive clips and wine choices

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: At Golitha Falls, where the River Fowey tumbles down to the sea, Rick meets Dawn French, who has made Cornwall her home. In the shadow of Devonport Dockyard, he boards the world's busiest chain ferry to cross the wide River Tamar, the ancient watery boundary between Devon and Cornwall - along the way discovering an unusual event involving circus elephant. And he meets a man who thinks he has discovered the next superfood, which Rick used to cook up a delicious duck dish

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the midweek's Premier League action and look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Liverpool v Manchester City

programme.title: Eddie Butler's Six Nations
programme.desc: Eddie Butler looks ahead to the 2021 Six Nations in the company of former Wales international Sam Warburton, and speaks to Eddie Jones, Serge Betsen, George North and Nigel Owens

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents as a team of gym enthusiasts take on a punk rock singer and an actress at Lincoln Antiques Fair, with help from experts Raj Bisram and Kate Bliss

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley assists a couple who have £375,000 to spend on a home in the north Dorset countryside, and while in the area, she visits the renowned Lyme Regis Boat Building Academy

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a garden in Loughborough with unsafe paving and a cherry tree with troublesome roots

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Walsall looking for two things to save from the clutches of the tip crusher. With the help of artisan Norman Wilkinson, Sarah hopes to turn a profit

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling. This time, Joel Dommett and Hannah Cooper Dommett take on Rickie Haywood Williams and Melvin Odoom

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces brave the black chair in another episode of the classic quiz where celebrities donate to their chosen charities. TV property expert Martin Roberts, author and TV presenter Fern Britton, actress and singer Victoria Ekanoye and TV presenter Arthur Williams answer questions on Thunderbirds, Mary of Teck, Pretty Woman and British Military Aircraft of the Second World War

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as former child stars take part in the general knowledge quiz, in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Adrian Hall, Julie Dawn Cole, Lyndyann Barrass, David Peachey, Nick Bond-Owen, Jessica Forte, Sally Thomsett and Tyger Drew-Honey

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Fenisha decides to tell Jan she is pregnant, but she needs to find the right moment. The pair are then called to a domestic abuse incident. Lev grapples with the truth that is tearing his family apart, and Leon makes a friend in David when he offers to fix his broken-down car

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: DI Lindsay Denton is investigated when she survives the ambush of a police convoy in which three of her colleagues are killed and a witness is seriously injured. AC-12's commanding officer Ted Hastings assigns new recruit Georgia Trotman to work alongside Steve Arnott on the investigation, and suspicions are soon raised about Denton. Starring Adrian Dunbar, Keeley Hawes, Martin Compston and Jessica Raine

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Arsenal at Villa Park and Manchester United v Everton at Old Trafford. The Villans and Gunners will both have been hoping to boost their chances of qualifying for Europe with a victory, while United continued their quest for the title with a potentially yough test aggainst the Toffees

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell look ahead to Tampa Bay Buccaneers v Kansas City Chiefs in Super Bowl LV at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida

programme.title: The Place Beyond the Pines (2012)
programme.desc: A motorcycle stunt rider is reunited with his lost love and the son he has never met. He gives up his life on the road to support them, but ends up drawn into crime, attracting the attention of a troubled, ambitious cop and setting in motion a conflict that will have unforeseen consequences for both men's children. Crime drama, starring Ryan Gosling and Bradley Cooper

programme.title: Seamus Heaney and the Music of What Happens
programme.desc: Documentary about the life and work of Irish poet Seamus Heaney, with his widow Marie and children Michael, Christopher and Catherine talking about their family life and reading some of the poems he wrote for them. His surviving brothers Hugh, Charlie, Colum and Dan remember their childhood and the shared experiences that inspired many of his finest poems

programme.title: Medbh McGuckian: Portrait of a Poet
programme.desc: An exploration of the life and poetry of Medbh McGuckian, telling the tales of her triumphs and hardships as she forged a life as one of Northern Ireland's foremost writers. Throughout the programme, Medbh's poems are brought to life through recitals and performances as she remembers the poems that defined her life and career

programme.title: Witness History
programme.desc: For this special edition of Witness History, Larry Madowo brings us five extraordinary stories to mark Black History Month from Washington DC. We hear about one of the darkest days in the civil rights era, when a baptist church was bombed, killing four young black girls, we spend some time at the Dance Theatre of Harlem - the first dance company in America to prioritise black dancers - and we remember the damning 1960s report that told the US it was fundamentally racist.

programme.desc: Artist Heather Dewar draws a charcoal portrait of Catrìona MacAskill on Eriskay

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Documentary about uses of water, including ice baths, cliff jumping and divination

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Gàrradh Phàdruig
programme.desc: It's nearing the end of the season, but there's still lots going on in the urban garden. Joy Dunlop comes to visit, and samples some homegrown treats

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: The story of a man from the Isle of Man who dedicated his life to documenting the folklore, poetry, music and songs of Scotland's highlands and islands

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads sample a variety of Middle Eastern dishes made by their Muslim neighbours, and are inspired to prepare their own Namak Mandi dish, chicken curry, and Tabbouleh

programme.title: DIY le Donnie
programme.desc: Donnie MacLeod builds a new roadway to a community polycrub in Bornish, South Uist, so those using it can access the agricultural shelter more easily. He also improves the rain water harvesting system by installing two tanks that will make watering the plants much easier for the members. The DIY expert also visits a manse renovation project in Point, Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Sgeul Slainte
programme.desc: A group of nurses gathers to discuss the job, share anecdotes and reflect on how the profession has changed since the Second World War

programme.title: TradFest
programme.desc: A selection of some of the best music from Tradfest Music Festival in Dublin, presented by Allan MacDonald. Showcasing brilliant musicians from Irish and traditional music, TradFest brings us a range of the best talent there is to find, with some of the biggest names in trad and Irish music. Amongst the performers are Eoghan Ó Ceannabháin, An teaghlach tàlantach, Mick, Aoife & Ciara O'Brien and Blazin' Fiddles

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Anna is practising for the school cycling test and is good at it. Dave isn't quite as skilled, but with a new bike from his parents he really hopes to pass.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: George takes the Spuds to the museum of mouldy cheese. Anna dares Dave to lick the cheeses on display.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A martian scientist gets lost in an underground cave, leading Virgil, Alan and Captain Taylor to assist

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's challenge will see him attempt to escape from a helicopter simulator underwater. Has he got what it takes to be submerged?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless buffoon gets a liking for painting. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The buffoon declares war when he finds a fly in his room, and searches for ever more outlandish weapons. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Tom and Jerry are hired by a British super spy to help guard the Queen's precious objects while they are on display in the city

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po and the Kids finally arrive in the Forbidden City - after an assassination scare, Xiao enlists Po and the Four Constellations for help protecting her during her father's funeral.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Good Morning Britain's Charlotte Hawkins, award-winning baker Richard Bertinet and chef Richard Corrigan, who will be preparing a Goan monkfish curry

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef presents a guide to cooking for friends, preparing cheese fondue, celeriac remoulade, and a delicate martini. Raymond is joined for dinner by fellow chef Nathan Outlaw

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef ventures into Louisiana's state capital Baton Rouge, to experience a unique American phenomenon. It's the day of a big football match at Louisiana State University, and the LSU Tigers are playing in front of thousands of fans. Surrounded by people barbecuing, cooking and sharing food from the back of their pick-up trucks, James joins in and cooks up a storm ahead of the game

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Italy v France (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the opening match of this season's championship, held at Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Italy have lost 27 consecutive matches in this competition since they won away to Scotland in 2015, and France will be determined to heap more misery on them after narrowly missing out on last season's title to England on points difference. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Maggie Alphonsi and Gareth Thomas, and commentary by Miles Harrison, David Flatman and Nolli Waterman

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: England v Scotland (Kick-off 4.45pm). All the action from both teams' opening match of the championship, as the rivals contest the Calcutta Cup at Twickenham. England were made to work hard for a 13-6 victory when the sides met at Twickenham last season, with Scotland still leading 3-0 with 10 minutes to go before Ellis Genge's try proved decisive, and Eddie Jones' men went on to win the title. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Jim Hamilton and Clive Woodward, pitchside reports by Martin Bayfield and commentary from Nick Mullins, Lawrence Dallaglio and Scott Hastings

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Joel Dommett hosts the semi-final of the singing contest, in which regular panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan are joined by special guest judge Matt Lucas as they attempt to uncover the identities of the mysterious performers. The show will feature a double elimination, as two of the five remaining contestants will be unmasked, leaving just three to go through to the final round

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the penultimate round of this year's blind auditions, with Anne-Marie joining the coaching panel alongside old hands Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am. The coaches will be judging tonight's contestants on their voice alone, listening out for that perfect performance that inspires them to spin their chairs

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. Team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett are joined by celebrity guests Tom Allen, Clare Balding, Russell Kane and AJ Odudu to compete head-to-head and test their TV knowledge

programme.title: Horrible Bosses (2011)
programme.desc: A power-crazed bully, a sleazy cocaine addict and a sexual predator each make an employee's life a misery, but the three workers cannot afford to quit their jobs. They resort to desperate measures by conspiring to murder each other's bosses, with a little help from an eccentric ex-convict. Comedy, starring Jason Bateman, Charlie Day, Jason Sudeikis, Kevin Spacey, Jennifer Aniston and Colin Farrell

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Entertainment news

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave rescues an endangered donkey from the docks. Hiding it in the camper van, things take a turn when the donkey eats Gran's glasses

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave is given a bulb to grow. Popping it in his lunch box, it isn't long before he discovers it's partial to Betty's sandwiches.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Lady Penelope and Parker compete in a desert motor race, and all seems well until the Chaos Crew steals an experimental new engine from the leading vehicle

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's in Blackpool but is he brave enough to walk to the top of the UK's tallest rollercoaster?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless bumbler is chased home by an angry mime artist. Voiced by Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless bumbler tries his hand at DIY plumbing. Voiced by Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: When an accident leaves Jerry wrapped head to toe in bandages and unable to take his scout troop camping a guilt-ridden Tom decides to lead the kids into the woods himself

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: When Babs and Kara spar over whose dog is better, they decide to settle it at the Metropolis Dog Show.

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: A brand-new immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter is in South Devon taking on a section of the South West Coast Path from the affluent coastal town of Salcombe to rugged Prawle Point. Here, she samples the world famous Salcombe crab, rambles the coast in search of wildlife with naturalist Claire, and meets one of the volunteers of the National Coast watch, who helps prevent shipwrecks on this dramatic and stunning stretch of coast

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Actor Jason Watkins talks detectives, and Alan also chats to star of Bridgerton, actress Adjoa Andoh, aka Lady Danbury. Plus, a feature on rabbits, the best in British vodka, and a look at the ancient art of trug making

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef presents a guide to cooking for friends, preparing cheese fondue, celeriac remoulade, and a delicate martini. Raymond is joined for dinner by fellow chef Nathan Outlaw

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team head to Essex to surprise selfless retired Nurse Pat Wade, who was the first person in the UK to set up a charity for young burn survivors - The Children's Burns Club. Alan, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney turn Pat's tiny concrete-covered backyard into an outdoor entertaining space - filled with flowers and bling

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. Highlights include a girl dropping a full spaghetti bolognese over her computer tablet, dashcam footage of a tree almost crushing a car and a selfie being spoiled by a naked photo-bomber's bum

programme.title: Uncle Buck (1989)
programme.desc: An unreliable but good-natured slob is called upon to look after his trouble-making nephew and nieces while their parents are away - and what starts as a disaster soon changes for the better as the unlikely babysitter gets to grips with the finer points of being a responsible adult. Family comedy, starring John Candy, Macaulay Culkin, Jean Kelly, Gaby Hoffman, Amy Madigan and Laurie Metcalf

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a Coronation Street edition of the quiz, as celebrity contestants Shelley King, Daniel Brocklebank, Kym Marsh and Jack P Shepherd pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby welcome the remaining 10 celebrities and their professional partners. They take on a variety of dance styles as they battle to stay in the competition. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean take to the ice for a performance and head up the ice panel, alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gives six new contestants the chance to take their place in the hot seat. Will any of them have what it takes to become a million-pound winner?

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: The widow is struggling to sleep, growing short of money, and Gerry has just accepted an offer on the house behind her back. In order to stop her home from being sold from under her, Alice must find a way to disrupt the sale and make big money fast. While Charlotte gets to know George, Alice uncovers a deed for land in Harry's name and wonders if this could be the lifeline she's been hoping for. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son continue their European adventure in Slovenia, where Brad's passion for bikes gives Barney an excuse to take his dad motocross racing. They meet a beekeeper and are treated to some unusual honey therapies, before joining the national quidditch team in the capital Ljubljana, and having a go at the notoriously dangerous sport of ski jumping

programme.title: The Chasers Road Trip: Trains, Brains and Automobiles
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty, Shaun Wallace and Mark Labbett's geeky road trip ends in Japan, where they uncover the surprising intelligence of robots. Travelling across the country, they come face to face with everything from a self-solving puzzle cube and facial recognition technology to cutting-edge robots that are being used in a wide variety of industries

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Yasika Fernando was separated from her Sri Lankan birth mother when she was adopted by a British couple at a few months old, and has longed to find her since finding out the truth at 18. The other story follows Richard Standen, who is looking for the son he put up for adoption more than 50 years ago

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents action from the opening weekend of the championship. Wales played host to Ireland at the Principality Stadium and Wayne Pivac's side will have wanted to break their run of four successive defeats in the Six Nations after a campaign to forget in 2020. Plus. the key moments from the Calcutta Cup, as England welcomed Scotland to Twickenham, and France's trip to Rome, as last year's runners-up took on Italy at the Stadio Olimpico

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Entertainment news

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Croft, featuring action from round 16 of the Ginetta Junior Championship and round four of the Mini Challenge

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as Elvira and her son Emmet from Bedford, London couple Charlie and Paul, and Matt and his son Eddie from Wiltshire use their powers of deduction to try to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Claudia, Neville, Kim and Pete pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gabby shows potential to Kim, Noah is annoyed by Charity's interference, and Matty and Amy arrange a date

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne lies to Nick, making out that Imran's keeping her job open for her and she is fine for money. Having failed to secure a mortgage, David and Shona are at their wit's end. Johnny is horrified when he fails to recognise a cry for help from Joe, a suicidal fellow prisoner

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The duo continue their European adventure in Italy, starting in the picturesque Lake Garda, where Barney surprises his dad by explaining they'll be running off the top of Monte Baldo and paragliding over the lake below. The next day Bradley is treated to a hair-raising ride in a Formula 1 speed boat, before the Walshes are invited to the home of opera superstar Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David and Tracy start a frenzied bidding war over Number 8, while Jenny is worried sick, aware that Joe's overdose will bring back dark thoughts of Aidan for Johnny. Debbie reveals to Ray's accountant, Miles, how she's no intention of allowing Crosby a new life in Turkey

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Claire Martin was abandoned in Hong Kong as a baby more than 50 years ago and heads back there to appeal for information about her origins, but with the help of DNA she finds family much closer to home. The team helps Nicki Goscomb and her sisters search for their brother, who was given up for adoption in 1966

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: After a lifetime of travels that have taken her across the globe, Joanna Lumley is making her most personal journey yet in the UK. Starting at Tilbury Docks in Essex she heads north, making a nostalgic return to Coronation Street, before ending the journey in Whitby

programme.title: Paul Sinha's TV Showdown
programme.desc: Chaser, stand-up comedian and TV trivia genius Paul Sinha hosts this TV-themed comedy quiz show. Team captains Fay Ripley and Rob Beckett are joined by celebrity guests Tom Allen, Clare Balding, Russell Kane and AJ Odudu to compete head-to-head and test their TV knowledge

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Entertainment news

programme.title: This Time Next Year
programme.desc: Davina McCall gives members of the public a chance to set themselves a deeply personal challenge, and records their progress as they seek to complete it within the space of 12 months. The series kicks off with a weight-loss special featuring people who, after a lifetime of bad habits, are finally ready to transform their health for the better by shedding the pounds. Davina meets a couple determined to lose 20st between them, a diabetic dad-of-four desperate to lose weight to avoid a lifetime of insulin injections, and an engaged nurse who has put off getting married for 17 years because she is terrified of being labelled a fat bride

programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team head to Essex to surprise selfless retired Nurse Pat Wade, who was the first person in the UK to set up a charity for young burn survivors - The Children's Burns Club. Alan, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney turn Pat's tiny concrete-covered backyard into an outdoor entertaining space - filled with flowers and bling

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. Highlights include a girl dropping a full spaghetti bolognese over her computer tablet, dashcam footage of a tree almost crushing a car and a selfie being spoiled by a naked photo-bomber's bum

programme.title: Uncle Buck (1989)
programme.desc: An unreliable but good-natured slob is called upon to look after his trouble-making nephew and nieces while their parents are away - and what starts as a disaster soon changes for the better as the unlikely babysitter gets to grips with the finer points of being a responsible adult. Family comedy, starring John Candy, Macaulay Culkin, Jean Kelly, Gaby Hoffman, Amy Madigan and Laurie Metcalf

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a Coronation Street edition of the quiz, as celebrity contestants Shelley King, Daniel Brocklebank, Kym Marsh and Jack P Shepherd pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby welcome the remaining 10 celebrities and their professional partners. They take on a variety of dance styles as they battle to stay in the competition. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean take to the ice for a performance and head up the ice panel, alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gives six new contestants the chance to take their place in the hot seat. Will any of them have what it takes to become a million-pound winner?

programme.title: Finding Alice
programme.desc: The widow is struggling to sleep, growing short of money, and Gerry has just accepted an offer on the house behind her back. In order to stop her home from being sold from under her, Alice must find a way to disrupt the sale and make big money fast. While Charlotte gets to know George, Alice uncovers a deed for land in Harry's name and wonders if this could be the lifeline she's been hoping for. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son continue their European adventure in Slovenia, where Brad's passion for bikes gives Barney an excuse to take his dad motocross racing. They meet a beekeeper and are treated to some unusual honey therapies, before joining the national quidditch team in the capital Ljubljana, and having a go at the notoriously dangerous sport of ski jumping

programme.title: The Chasers Road Trip: Trains, Brains and Automobiles
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty, Shaun Wallace and Mark Labbett's geeky road trip ends in Japan, where they uncover the surprising intelligence of robots. Travelling across the country, they come face to face with everything from a self-solving puzzle cube and facial recognition technology to cutting-edge robots that are being used in a wide variety of industries

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Yasika Fernando was separated from her Sri Lankan birth mother when she was adopted by a British couple at a few months old, and has longed to find her since finding out the truth at 18. The other story follows Richard Standen, who is looking for the son he put up for adoption more than 50 years ago

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents action from the opening weekend of the championship. Wales played host to Ireland at the Principality Stadium and Wayne Pivac's side will have wanted to break their run of four successive defeats in the Six Nations after a campaign to forget in 2020. Plus. the key moments from the Calcutta Cup, as England welcomed Scotland to Twickenham, and France's trip to Rome, as last year's runners-up took on Italy at the Stadio Olimpico

programme.desc: A book dealer is found drowned, having seemingly committed suicide in despair at having sunk all his cash in a local inventor's revolutionary new transport system - but the discovery of a second body makes it clear that there is a murderer at large. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local woman is found murdered following a row with her husband at a residents' association Spanish-themed evening. The finger of suspicion soon points to an odd-job man with a violent history, and the chairman of the association reveals he heard a squeaking noise after the victim left - but the witness promptly winds up dead himself. Crime drama, starring John Nettles

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A depressive gambler is found dead, the apparent victim of suicide. Dr Jane Moore, who once treated the man, is adamant he wouldn't have taken his own life - a theory backed up by the post-mortem. Thrilled to be working with Jane again, Barnaby investigates the case - and it isn't long before another of the doctor's patients is killed. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Isla Blair, Jan Ravens and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called in when David Kenworthy, the son of a prominent local family, is found murdered on the beach at Whitley Bay. A witness spotted arcade owner Larry Crowe arguing with David on the night of the killing, and a search of the suspect's property uncovers a bloodied baseball bat. However, Larry has a cast-iron alibi and it's clear he wasn't the only person the victim met that night. A second death then leaves Vera facing mounting pressure to crack the case, and struggling to answer the question of why David was drawn to the seafront on the evening of his father's retirement. Brenda Blethyn stars in the murder mystery, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A magistrate acquaintance of Wycliffe is found hanged and all the evidence points to suicide, but the Cornish copper remains unconvinced and pursues an unorthodox investigation, eventually forcing the forensic pathologist to reconsider his verdict. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Pam Ferris

David Jason stars as the grouchy inspector, with Tony Haygarth, Diane Bull, Denise Black and Dominic Jephcott

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. It's the start of the new year, and after their busy festive period, the hotel has high hopes for 2020 - looking at how they can improve all areas of their service and make it the most successful year in the hotel's history. Head butler Sean is determined to make his newest recruit - 37-year-old former actor Michael - the perfect Savoy butler, and is putting him to the test. As well as having to juggle a multitude of requests, Sean wants Michael to host an exclusive cocktail party for regular VIP guests, Mr and Mrs Cuming, in their suite

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The body of a man killed by a single gunshot wound is discovered on the moors and a burnt-out truck containing the remains of a deer is found a mile away. Joe is convinced the case is a poaching war gone wrong, but for Vera, the truth is never that straightforward. Delving into the victim's story, she unearths a sad childhood in the country and a troubled life in the city, scraping by as a novelist. Following the recent death of his grandfather, he returned to Northumberland after a 15-year absence to sell his inheritance to a wealthy couple - but pulled out of the deal at the last minute. Could his death be linked to a dispute over land? Brenda Blethyn and David Leon star in the murder mystery

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A blackmailer contaminates a jar of baby food in a local supermarket and promises it will not be the last if he does not receive payment. Wycliffe and his team spring into action, aware they are under investigation by the police disciplinary squad. Crime drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks into a lone yachtswoman's mysterious disappearance. He learns she had been receiving threats from the owners of a local china clay works - where he later makes a grisly discovery. Jack Shepherd stars, with Con O'Neill and Michael Attwell

programme.desc: A dark family secret comes to light when a female hit-and-run suspect claims the woman she deliberately ran over on a pedestrian crossing was a ghost. However, the facts of the case tell a different story. Gwen Taylor guest stars, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth.
